vol_name: Spatial Learning in Play (SLIP) at Cornell
vol_description: The videos are short tasks in which children are assessed on their spatial skills, numerical knowledge, and engage with a caregiver in either play tasks or book reading.

The videos are parent-child spatial play. Parents and children created a pattern with a magnet board at baseline and then again at posttest. Dyads were randomly assigned to take either spatial or non-spatial toys home to use for 4 weeks.
vol_short_name: Play at Home and Spatial Skills

vol_description: SLIP data from the Ithaca sample

vol_name: Shape Up: Parent and Child Spatial Play
vol_description: The data are videos of parent-child interactions during a play task.
The tasks, videos, and data for the various studies of labeling and elicited narration.

vol_name: Parental Beliefs about Infant and Child Learning
vol_description: This study was a pilot examining parental beliefs about play. We also examine how well parents adopt scaffolding behaviors following a brief training. Data was collected in NYC in 2018.

vol_name: Transfer of spatial relations from 2D-3D with varied relational language
vol_description: Video recordings of 3-3.5 year old children completing a touchscreen task involving spatial relations and varied relational language. Task includes both a 2D-2D test and a 2D-3D test.

vol_name: Children’s Exposure to Spatial Language Promotes Their Spatial Thinking
vol_description:
- 5 play sessions
- 4 year olds assigned to "play-only" or "spatial language and play" condition

vol_name: Training Spatial Skills Through Motor vs. Spatial Language
vol_description: This training study examined the effects of a motor versus a spatial language training on preschool children's spatial skills and spatial vocabulary.

Home visit video data sessions collected as part of Cornell's contributions to the NIH-funded Play and Learning Across a Year (PLAY) Project. Session data consist of the following:
-Video files of 1-hour natural play, 5-minute structured play, house walk-through, and questionnaires
-Responses to questionnaires covering the participant’s demographics, language, temperament, and locomotion
-Ambient noise decibel meter recordings captured during the 1-hour natural play

vol_name: Parent and Infant Play and Parental Beliefs
vol_description: Several of our studies, funded by the USDA, have examined infants or preschool-aged children interacting with a caregiver during play. For some studies, we have invited parents to share their beliefs about play and learning. In one study, parents played with their infant of 6 to 18 months in their home and the play was recorded via Zoom. Parents selected which toys they and their infant would have available during the play session. In another study, parents were sent materials via the mail and completed three geometric patterns with their preschool-aged child at home. These sessions were also recorded via Zoom. In a third set of studies, caregivers and their preschool-aged child completed a geometric pattern in the lab over two sessions, about one month apart.
